pattern: Working top to bottom, each row only needs enough trailing zeros for its position, so greedily pulling up the closest qualifying row below with adjacent swaps is always optimal.
hints: Precompute, for every row, how many zeros trail it from the right — that count is what determines which position a row is allowed to sit in.
Process rows top to bottom: a row already has enough trailing zeros for its position, or it needs to borrow a better row from further down.
Search downward for the closest row with enough trailing zeros, bubble it up one adjacent swap at a time (each swap counts as one operation), and give up with -1 the moment no such row exists below.

edge_inputs.description: already valid grid needs no swaps
single cell grid is trivially valid
identical rows can never satisfy the diagonal condition
the qualifying row sits far from where it is needed

stress.description: large random binary grid, some instances unsolvable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
